---
title: "It Worked!!"
date: "2026-05-21"
description: "The moment my Quarto blog finally went live — and how it felt."
categories: ["blog", "personal"]
---

Tonight, something small but meaningful happened:\
**my blog went live.**

There’s a very specific feeling you get the first time you deploy something you built yourself. It’s a mix of relief, disbelief, and a quiet little “holy crap, I actually did it.”

After wrestling with Quarto, GitHub, GitHub Actions, branches, caches, and a few cryptic error messages, I finally saw the thing anyone hopes to see after trying to publish something online:\
**a green tick.**\
Then another one.\
And suddenly… my blog was live.

What made it even better was the tab I had open right next to it:\
**Hadley Wickham’s website.**

Seeing my own Quarto‑powered site come alive while his sat in the next tab felt poetic — like I’d just stepped into the same open‑source publishing ecosystem he helped build.

This blog is simple right now. A few posts, a clean layout, nothing fancy. But it’s mine. It lives on the internet. And it works.

If you’re reading this, welcome.\
This is the beginning — of writing, sharing, building in public, and documenting the things I learn and enjoy along the way.

And if you’re someone staring at a red ❌ in GitHub Actions wondering if you’ll ever see green… trust me, you will. And when you do, it feels good!!
